Porting policies and processes
When porting, NatWest pays a procuration fee on the full application amount, not just the top up. A minimum loan size of £25,000 applies to qualify for a procuration fee.
When porting you can change the borrowers, repayment method & term without incurring an ERC (early repayment charge) subject to meeting lending criteria.
The existing mortgage needs to be redeemed but the existing property does not necessarily need to be sold i.e. can be repaid from savings or remortgaged with another lender.
Some Right to Buy, Green and shared equity products are not portable as standard and may require an exception. Please contact our BDA team to arrange this.

The existing LTV of the ported rate will not apply for the new purchase e.g. we can consider a customer that has a 70% LTV rate and is purchasing a property at 90% LTV (any additional borrowing on the new deal will be based on the new LTV rate).
It's acceptable for a customer to sell two properties (under the same brand) and port the rate(s) over to a new property.
The customer can port their mortgage to a new property providing they have made their first mortgage payment.

Like-for-Like or less borrowing for a residential port doesn’t need to pass affordability, however where the Loan To Value has increased we must have confirmation that this is in the customers best interests and will remain an underwriting decision. Please see Apply for a port in just 6 easy steps in how to provide that within the application to avoid any delays.

Where the AIP has declined for a like-for-like or less borrowing residential port, this can also be considered if it is in the customer’s best interests and remains an underwriting decision. Please contact our BDA team to discuss this further.
We only accept a Split term for mixed repayment methods (one part on Capital & Interest and one part on Interest Only).

For existing mortgages with split/multiple terms, these will remain acceptable where a Like-for-Like or reduced balance port is being undertaken. Any other changes will require the terms to be aligned to one term per repayment method.
We can only port a rate where the existing mortgage has not already been redeemed on or before the date they are applying for the new mortgage.
Products can only be ported to purchase a property, the customer can't port to a property they already own.

Where the mortgage is currently joint, the product can’t be split and must be on one property. If there is more than one product, they can both port a different product into a new property each, providing written consent is given as per below.
Where a borrower is changing (e.g. joint to sole) we require written consent from the party being removed is needed from the outset. This should confirm:
They are happy for the other party to port this mortgage product.
They will not be porting this rate themselves.

Where an Early Redemption Charge (ERC) is paid when the existing mortgage is redeemed, confirmation they are aware this will be fully refunded to the person porting the rate once the new mortgage completes.
The following definitions are classed as like-for-like on porting applications:
Borrowing amount the same or less.
Term the same or longer.
LTV the same or lower.
Repayment type staying the same.
Applicants staying the same.
Where a customer is porting and requires additional funds of £10,000 or more, they will be eligible to take a New Business Rate on the additional funds.
Where a customer is porting and requires additional funds of £9,999 or less then the additional funds will be on the NatWest Standard Variable Rate (SVR).
There is no minimum balance required to allow a customer to port i.e. a balance under £25,000 can be ported.

If customers reduce their ported balance then a partial ERC must be paid for the amount that the borrowing is being reduced by, less any overpayment allowance, if it is tied into a product at the time of redemption.
There are no valuation fees payable on a port, even if the customer is not topping up the borrowing on a new business product. See Free Valuations Products for any exclusions.

If the customer is already on SVR they can port their mortgage, but any additional funds borrowed need to be taken on an alternative product. If the requested additional funds are below £10,000, then the customer must reduce their SVR balance so that the balance on the alternative product is £10,000.

source_text: Product transfer policies and processes

section: Your customers could secure a new mortgage with NatWest 4 months before their current deal ends.
source_ref: Your customers could secure a new mortgage with NatWest 4 months before their current deal ends. item 1803
source_text: Your customers could secure a new mortgage with NatWest 4 months before their current deal ends.
You can secure a new rate for your customer 4 months before their current deal ends.
Quick, straight forward processing. There is no additional underwriting for standalone product transfers.
Ability to change the product before completion.
Customers can apply for Additional Borrowing at the same time (subject to underwriting).
Available for residential and Buy to Let customers who are:
Within their roll-off period. (4 months before their current deal ends).
On a standard variable rate (SVR).
Within the track and switch eligibility period on a tracker rate.
The minimum loan amount to secure a new rate is £10,000.
More than 27 months term remaining at the date of applying for their product switch (end date of product must be before end date of mortgage).
Customers will need to contact us directly where they:
Want to exit an existing deal ahead of the roll-off period.
Want to change their mortgage term or repayment type.
Are in mortgage arrears.
Have more than 5 eligible to switch sub accounts.
Log in to our website.
Enter the customer’s details: Mortgage Account number, Surname, Property Postcode and Date of Birth.

The HPI and Current Valuation figures are available on screen and the higher of these figures is used to calculate the LTV. If the valuation figure is not showing then you must call us to get this updated.
If the customer is eligible to switch, you will be able to choose a new deal from the rates available which will be displayed on the screen.

Once you have finished the application, the next steps will be displayed on the screen. In some circumstances, documents may take up to five working days to be emailed over to the customer and yourself. If this is the case then the on screen guidance will tell you this.
Customers must accept the offer within 14 calendar days, or the system will automatically cancel their application.

Customer’s need to be registered for online banking to have access to enhanced security and all of the additional services available for our mortgage customers. To accept the rate, they need to log in to Manage your Mortgage via the mobile app or online banking, they will be emailed the steps when their offer is ready to accept. If your customer needs further support in how to register for online banking or how to accept the rate if they are already registered, please use the relevant support pack available below, which you can download and forward directly to your customer.

Once the acceptance has been received, the new rate will take effect when the existing fixed rate expires. Where the existing rate has already expired or where a customer is switching to a fixed rate as part of a track and switch application, the new rate will start as soon as possible after the signed acceptance has been received.

Where there is more than one sub-account that you are looking to switch and they have different product expiry dates, you will submit these as one application. Internally we will then manually key these as separate applications. This means we will send the paperwork for the sub account that is due to expire first and once the rate is live on that sub-account, we will then send the paperwork for the next sub account(s). You do not need to submit any further applications for the eligible sub accounts you have selected.
Support packs available to aid with acceptance if necessary:
Registering for online banking
Registering for the mobile app
Accepting via online banking
Accepting via mobile app
For customers to access further Support they can visit our Login help page. Here they will find Troubleshooting & FAQs such as “ I've forgotten my Online Banking login details, what should I do?”
Valuations
When you log in to apply for a product transfer, you will see the current valuation and also the HPI.
We will use the higher of the valuation or the HPI to calculate the Loan to Value.

Customers can request a standard valuation where they choose to do so. There is a fee applicable for this. For costs please see ‘valuation fees’ on our property and valuation hub . The customer will need to call 0345 302 0190 (Relay UK: 18001 0345 302 0190) to arrange this.

If a standard valuation is required, this can add additional time to the process. The new valuation will replace the existing valuation, even if it is lower. Once the valuation has been carried out and updated you can then apply for the product transfer application in the portal.

source_text: We can look to lend on a range of different property types including ex-local authority homes, properties above or near commercial premises and freehold flats.
The property must be:
Readily saleable & mortgageable for owner occupation
In England, Wales, Scotland, Northern Ireland and the Isles of Scilly.

Where a property has been built, converted or significantly renovated within the last 10 years, we will require a warranty to be in place. Please see our New Build Hub for acceptable warranties and further guidance.

We will base the minimum property value on the purchase price or the valuation figure, whichever is lower.

We don’t have a maximum plot size.
Where a property is over 4 hectares/10 acres, we will be able to consider the property subject to underwriter assessment and a suitable valuation report.

Properties with an annex may be considered subject to valuer’s comments, where it is intended that a family member will reside in the annex or where this will be used as family staff accommodation e.g. live-in childcare arrangements. In addition to standard valuation requirements, the valuer will consider the following when assessing the property:
Potential for letting: We can't lend on a property where the annex could be sublet on a permanent or temporary basis.

Access and service: An annex should normally share its access and services with the main residence. If these are separate the risk of the annex being sublet is increased and may result in the property being declined.

Size and nature: The annex should be secondary to the main residence. For example a 6 bedroom house with a 1 bedroom annex could be considered however a 3 bedroom house with a 2 bedroom cottage is likely to be unacceptable.

Buy to let: Where the property will be a buy to let, this can only be considered where there is likely to be sufficient demand from potential tenants who wish to occupy the whole property as a single- family residence.
We treat coach houses the same as ‘Freehold Flats/Flying Freehold Properties’, please see this section for full details.

source_text: Solicitors have a duty of care to ensure that any leasehold agreement is not onerous for the customer.
NatWest will be advised by either the valuer or the Solicitor of any restrictions, often these will be detailed in a Section 106 agreement (Section 75 in Scotland).

Please note, certain restrictions may still apply to Shared Ownership. We have specific guidance which can be found in the following document under England and Wales > Shared Ownership Requirements > Restrictions on the Disposal of the property in a Section 106 Agreement (or equivalent). The restrictions we can consider are:
Retirement complexes where there is a restriction in the age of the owner or occupier, for example must be over age 50.
Properties where occupation is restricted to only part of the year, for example 10/11 months provided the customer is using the property as a holiday/2nd home.

Right to Buy - properties may have a restriction in the title whereby the owner has to offer the property back to the local council if selling This restriction lasts for 10 years As NatWest are supportive of the Right to Buy scheme.
Agricultural Restrictions:
Only considered acceptable on residential applications
Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) is 50%
There should be no evidence of agricultural or commercial activity.
The applicant must be employed in an agricultural related job. However, their primary source of income must not be related to the mortgaged property.
The restrictions we can’t consider:
An overage clause which may entitle the previous vendor to a share of the profit of a subsequent sale of part (or whole) of the property.
Restrictions determining who can buy the property, for example people on a housing waiting list, people who have lived in the area for a certain period, people of certain professions.
Restrictions on the selling price, e.g. the property must be sold for 20% under market value.
This is not a definitive list of unacceptable restrictions.

Asbestos.

Flooding and coastal erosion.
Geological problems such as the presence of gases, coal and other forms of mining operations (past and present) etc.
Invasion plants, including Japanese Knotweed.
The valuer must assess each case on its individual merits. They may request specialist reports to support them with their assessment.

We rely upon the professional opinion of our valuers to confirm if a property shows the presence of potentially combustible cladding or if there are other fire safety concerns. Should they feel this is the case, where confirmation of remediation is not available through Government Remediation data or an EWS1/FRAEW is not already available for a building, the valuer may request an EWS1 form or FRAEW (Fire Risk Assessment of External Walls).
All valuations remain subject to valuer’s comments.
For flats in buildings over 11m within England, we will check Government Remediation Data to confirm whether the property is:
•Eligible for funding under Government Remediation Schemes
•Eligible for funding under Developer Remediation Schemes or assessed as not requiring remediation
Where this is confirmed, no EWS1 form or Leaseholder Deed of Certificate will be required and the property will be acceptable subject to the valuer’s comments.
Where an EWS1 form / FRAEW is requested:
•Flats in buildings with an EWS1 rating A1, A2 or B1 or FRAEW confirming remediation isn't required, are acceptable as standard
•Flats in building over 11m within England with an EWS1 rating A3 or B2 or FRAEW confirming remediation is required are acceptable subject to a qualifying leaseholder deed of certificate.
When leaseholder deed of certificates are required on a purchase, these would need to be obtained from the Vendor.
Where an EWS1 form / FRAEW is requested:
•Flats in buildings with an EWS1 rating A1, A2 or B1 or FRAEW confirming remediation isn't required, are acceptable as standard
•Flats in building over 11m within Wales with an EWS1 rating A3 or B2 or FRAEW confirming remediation is required are acceptable and will be confirmed by the valuation report.
Where an EWS1 form / FRAEW is requested:
•Flats in buildings with an EWS1 rating A1, A2 or B1 or FRAEW confirming remediation isn't required, are acceptable as standard.
•Flats in buildings with an EWS1 rating A3 or B2 or FRAEW confirming remediation is required, are not currently acceptable.

Where an EWS1/FRAEW is not available, we may accept alternative forms of remediation evidence on a case by case basis. Alternative forms of remediation evidence include a letter of comfort from the developer or DLUHC (Department of Levelling Up Housing and Communities), in addition to a Leaseholder Deed of Certificate.
The FRAEW must:
• Contain an executive Summary which clearly advises if remediation is required or if an EWS1 rating has been provided.
• Be signed and have the signatories qualifications listed.
• The building name and postcode on the FRAEW must match the application.
Please note: A fire risk assessment (FRA) is not a specialist report for external walls and will not be accepted.
Valuations
Remortgage- We provide a free valuation on properties valued up to £10 million.
Purchase- We provide a free valuation on properties where the lower of the purchase price or value is less than or equal to £3 million.
The free valuation is applicable per property, rather than per application.

We will complete our own checks to make sure a property meets our lending criteria. This may not be a physical inspection of the property. We may use an Automated Valuation Model (AVM) or complete a desktop valuation. This check is for the banks use only. The customer will not receive a copy of the valuation report.
We highly recommend that your customer considers carrying out their own checks to satisfy themselves of the property value and condition before completing on a purchase.
You can find more information on mortgage valuations here .
A valuation will always be obtained using a panel surveyor instructed by us (NatWest).
Where a valuation fee is payable, the fees are:
Up to £3 million £102
£3 million - £10 million £1380
This fee is based on the lower of the purchase price or estimated value of the property.

The payment will be taken from the card details supplied on the application form. If for any reason, payment can't be processed from these details the customer or broker will have to call 0345 600 0205 and make the payment.
For any properties valued over £10 million, valuations are available upon request via Legal and General Surveying Services.

Where a valuation fee is applicable, we will provide customers with a full valuation fee refund if their applications are declined on the grounds of affordability or credit scoring or where we can't lend the full amount requested due to affordability issues (unless they have requested to instruct a valuation at their own risk i.e a purchase application where the property value is over £3 million).

Valuations are instructed on Day 1. For applications where the purchase price is above £3 million or the property value is over £10 million (remortgage applications), our processing team will contact you prior to the valuation instruction and we adhere to the following process:

Where the valuation fee is applicable, the customer is given the choice between whether the valuation is instructed at that point at the customers own risk following receipt of application or wait until the case is fully agreed by an underwriter (Please note that if the customer does proceed and the valuation is instructed at customers own risk, they could lose the fee if the valuation is carried out but they withdraw.)
Please consult our Service Levels page for current processing timescales.
Valuation challenges will now only be accepted where it meets one of the following criteria:
1) Factual error on the valuation instruction e.g. number of bedrooms noted is incorrect. Please contact us to make us aware of any errors.

2) The property is a New Build and there are relevant recent comparables on the same site, along with evidence of second hand properties and/or other new build properties on a separate development. For further guidance and to raise a valuation challenge, please see our L&G New Build Valuation Challenge Form.

source_text: Looking for new ways to give your customers more choice and flexibility with their mortgages? NatWest’s remortgage options could make it easy to offer something extra.
Free valuations as standard.

Product options:
Valuation fee paid for properties valued up to £10 million AND
£350 cashback option OR
Standard legal fees paid up to £2m loan size - includes unencumbered properties. We’ll cover standard remortgage legal fees, with any additional Solicitors' fees being met by the customer.
Additional borrowing is available when your customer remortgages. We can do additional borrowing up to 90% LTV (Restricted to 80% LTV where there is Debt Consolidation )
We can help with Help to Buy remortgages where the loan remains or where this is being repaid in full.
We can help with Day one Consent to Let for Armed forces remortgaging from another lender.

A remortgage is a transfer of an existing mortgage from one lender to another, where there is no house move involved. This also applies to properties with no existing mortgage (known as an unencumbered property). Customers can do additional borrowing when they remortgage with us.
Remortgage criteria

We can support remortgage applications where the customer has owned their property for less than six months, in eligible scenarios. This flexibility helps you place cases where timelines or circumstances fall outside traditional ownership patterns – see our Day-1 remortgage guidance for more information.
£1,000 Balance Tolerance

To help avoid last-minute delays, we allow a tolerance of up to £1,000 where the final redemption figure differs from the loan amount requested at completion.‑minute delays, we allow a tolerance of up to £1,000 where the final redemption figure differs from the loan amount requested at completion.
As long as the LTV remains within the selected product’s LTV limit and does not increase into a higher LTV band, the case proceeds without re‑underwriting, additional checks, or broker action.
How it works
Differences up to £1,000 are handled directly by the solicitor
The solicitor requests the tolerance where needed
Your original advice remains valid, helping avoid unexpected changes
This tolerance provides reassurance that small redemption variances won’t disrupt your customer’s completion.
Let-to-Buy Remortgages

In a limited company the business is legally separated from its owners (shareholders) and managers (directors). It must be incorporated at Companies House.

This type of structure limits the amount of liability the shareholders and directors have to their stake in the company, which means that the finances of the people who run it are separate from the personal finances of the shareholders or directors.

A limited company can keep any profits it makes after paying tax. This means that if a shareholder or director of a limited company applies for a mortgage, they are only able to use the income that the limited company has paid them in salary or dividends (return on shares).
Limited company directors with a shareholding of 20% or more of the company are assessed as self-employed.
We’ll need confirmation of the last 2 years salary/Directors Remuneration and dividends:
Where the latest year’s income has remained the same or increased, we will use an average.
Where the income has decreased, we will use the latest year.
Income has increased so we will average.

Limited company accounts must be submitted to Companies House annually so they can be viewed by any member of the public. Most Limited Company accounts must be filed within nine months of the accounting reference date to avoid penalties. A company can change its accounting reference date, sometimes this means a shorter accounting period than the standard 12 months.
For information on customers who own less than 20% of a company and receive Dividend, please see the Income & Packaging Guide .
To check what packaging we will need to see, please visit our Income and Packaging hub > Self-employed ​
Important notes:
The business should be making a profit and be solvent.
The dividend payments should not exceed the net profit after tax. We will not accept income from retained profit or directors’ loans (money being repaid to the director by the company).
If the company shows less profit than normal due to a one-off extraordinary acquisition (eg machinery) please supply a written letter of explanation from the company accountant.
Audited accounts are acceptable if they’re prepared by a person who is suitably qualified and a member of an organisation; for example chartered accountant or certified accountant who belongs to:
Association of Authorised Public Accountants.
Institute of Financial Accountants.
Chartered Institute of Taxation.
Chartered Public Accountants.
Association of Accounting Technicians.
Chartered Institute of Management Accountants.
Association of Taxation Technicians.
Institute of Chartered Secretaries & Administrators.
Association of International Accountants.
Please note that this is not an exhaustive list and other appropriate professional bodies are acceptable.

section: Benefits and considerations of Shared Ownership
source_ref: Benefits and considerations of Shared Ownership item 2278
source_text: Benefits and considerations of Shared Ownership
Customers don’t need to buy the whole home at once They could start with a smaller share, which means a smaller deposit and mortgage.
It could be more affordable Customers are only buying part of the home, their monthly costs could be lower than buying outright.
They could buy more later If your customer's money situation changes, they could buy more shares over time. (Staircasing).
Available for both buying a new home and remortgaging Whether they're getting on the property ladder or switching their current Shared Ownership mortgage, this option gives them flexibility.
They are still a homeowner Even if customers own just part of it, it’s theirs. They can live in the home and make it their own.

Customers will pay rent and mortgage​ They will pay rent on the part they don’t own, as well as their mortgage payments. When we calculate affordability, we’ll use either the payment entered or 3% of the share the customer doesn't own, whichever is higher.
There may be extra costs Like service charges or fees when they buy more shares (staircasing).
Customers will need to sell their share if they move They can’t rent it out or keep it — they'll need to sell their share if they want to leave.

Customers can port their mortgage rate when moving from a Shared Ownership property to a Non-Shared Ownership property. Shared Ownership/Co-Ownership mortgages can't be ported between Shared Ownership properties. Porting from a Non-Shared Ownership property into Shared Ownership is also not permitted.
Consent to let will not be available.
Only registered scheme providers will be accepted.
Loan to Income will be capped at 4.45x.
Minimum share is 25%.
The lease must allow staircasing up to 100% of the value of the property.
Not every home is available through Shared Ownership. It depends on what housing associations and developers are offering in your customer's area.

Northern Ireland (covers most providers but not all)

Track and switch information

section: What is our Track and Switch facility?
source_ref: What is our Track and Switch facility? item 2326
source_text: Our Track and Switch facility allows customers who are uncertain about fixing their rate right now to choose a Tracker mortgage and fix their mortgage rate later.

section: What is our Track and Switch facility?
source_ref: What is our Track and Switch facility? item 2327
source_text: It’s simple.

section: What is our Track and Switch facility?
source_ref: What is our Track and Switch facility? item 2328
source_text: If it’s right for your customer, they can take any Tracker mortgage now. NatWest Tracker mortgages are variable rate products which track the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate. You’ll get paid your standard procurement fee for any new or existing business tracker cases.

section: What is our Track and Switch facility?
source_ref: What is our Track and Switch facility? item 2329
source_text: Then, if they wish to switch to a Fixed Rate mortgage, you can use our Product Transfer system to switch them easily onto the Fixed Rate they’d like. They can do this once their tracker mortgage has drawn down, they won’t be re-credit scored and you'll receive a standard Product Transfer procuration fee.
Frequently asked questions

source_ref: What is a Tracker mortgage? item 2331
source_text: A Tracker mortgage is a product with a variable rate that tracks the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate. This means that the customers mortgage rate and amount they pay for their mortgage on a monthly basis can change. An increase could see the rate your customer pays increase too. Likewise, if the rate goes down, the rate your customer pays should follow

section: What is the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate?
source_ref: What is the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate? item 2332
source_text: What is the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate?

section: What is the difference between the Bank of England Base Rate and the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate?
source_ref: What is the difference between the Bank of England Base Rate and the National Westminster Bank Plc base rate? item 2333
source_text: The Bank of England is the central bank of the United Kingdom and their job is to ensure a stable and strong economy. They set their own base rate which in turn influences the rate lenders are able to offer for products like mortgages. Changes in the Bank of England Base Rate are one of the main reasons we change our National Westminster Bank Plc Base Rate, but it is not the only reason. We have full control over our own base rate and this could go up or down regardless of what happens to the Bank of England base rate

source_ref: What is Track and Switch? item 2334
source_text: Track and Switch is a facility that allows a customer to take a Tracker mortgage now and switch onto a Fixed Rate mortgage later. The customer will not be re-credit checked or scored.

source_ref: What is Track and Switch? item 2335
source_text: The customer can switch onto a Natwest fixed rate after the tracker mortgage has drawn down. To see what rates are available to your customer, you can log in using their details and we’ll show you what deals you can choose from today. All Tracker products may be switched to a Natwest fixed rate product without incurring the Early Repayment Charge, subject to meeting the new product criteria and availability at the time of application. The product switch is subject to any fee(s) applicable to the new product at that time. The new fixed rate product may also have an Early Repayment Charge.

source_ref: What is Track and Switch? item 2336
source_text: Please note: If your customer is on a Lifetime Tracker rate, they will need to contact us directly to apply for a product transfer.

Providing the customer has drawn down on their tracker mortgage, when the customer wants to switch on to a Fixed Rate product, this works like a Product Transfer. Terms apply. There is no additional credit scoring or underwriting. The customer can choose any product providing they meet the criteria for the product i.e. LTV, remaining term etc.

It is for customers who want to take advantage of a Tracker mortgage now but may want to fix their mortgage rate later. It is for those customers who are comfortable with a variable rate and payment which can change monthly.

section: How long does my customer have to be on a Tracker mortgage before they can fix their rate?
source_ref: How long does my customer have to be on a Tracker mortgage before they can fix their rate? item 2341
source_text: The customer needs to be drawn down on the tracker product before they can switch into a Fixed Rate mortgage.

source_ref: How do I do this? item 2342
source_text: Once your customer has drawn down onto their Tracker mortgage you will be able to switch them via our Product Transfer system.

section: How do I get paid for a new business customer?
source_ref: How do I get paid for a new business customer? item 2343
source_text: If the customer is a new customer to NatWest when you recommend a Tracker mortgage you will be paid your standard procuration fee. If your customer chooses to switch to a Fixed Rate mortgage you’ll be paid for doing a product transfer. Please check your own or your network and clubs’ terms for the latest procuration fees.

section: How will this work for a Product Transfer?
source_ref: How will this work for a Product Transfer? item 2344
source_text: If the customer is doing a Product Transfer with NatWest and you recommend a Tracker mortgage, you will be paid your Product Transfer procuration fee. If your customer then chooses to switch to a Fixed Rate mortgage you’ll be paid a Product Transfer procuration fee. Please check your own or your network and clubs’ terms for the latest procuration fees.

section: Will switching incur an Early Repayment Charge?
source_ref: Will switching incur an Early Repayment Charge? item 2345
source_text: All Tracker products may be switched to any NatWest fixed rate product, subject to meeting the new product criteria and availability at the time of application and without incurring the Early Repayment Charge.

source_ref: Can customers on a track and switch deal, switch to a fixed rate at the same time they port their mortgage? item 2346
source_text: A customer can switch to a fixed deal without incurring an Early Repayment Charge while porting.

section: Is Additional Borrowing allowed when switching to a fixed rate?
source_ref: Is Additional Borrowing allowed when switching to a fixed rate? item 2350
source_text: Yes, they can apply as early as after the 1st months payment on their new mortgage.

section: What happens in a product transfer if the customer is in the rolloff window?
source_ref: What happens in a product transfer if the customer is in the rolloff window? item 2351
source_text: If the customer’s tracker deal is coming to an end and they have entered the roll off window, you will be given the option to start the deal straight away or when the customers current deal ends. There will be no ERC charged for switching immediately. This includes tracker to tracker products if available. (Tracker to tracker is only available within the roll off window)

section: My client is not yet within their product transfer window but wants additional borrowing. They’d like both products to have aligned end dates — what are the options?
source_ref: My client is not yet within their product transfer window but wants additional borrowing. They’d like both products to have aligned end dates — what are the options? item 2352
source_text: You can consider our Track & Switch facility. This allows the additional borrowing to be placed on a tracker product initially. Once the client enters their product transfer window, both the existing mortgage and the additional borrowing can be switched to a new fixed rate product with aligned end dates. Please ensure you recommend the most suitable option based on your client's individual needs and circumstances.

section: If I have secured a new rate to start when the current tracker deal ends, can I bring the start date of that deal forward?
source_ref: If I have secured a new rate to start when the current tracker deal ends, can I bring the start date of that deal forward? item 2353
source_text: No. It you want to start a new deal immediately then you need to cancel the application in full and reapply.

section: Can I delay the start date of the new deal if I am doing a product transfer using the track and switch facility?
source_ref: Can I delay the start date of the new deal if I am doing a product transfer using the track and switch facility? item 2354
source_text: No. If you are using the track and switch facility the new deal will start immediately.
